_meta.purpose: CT-B exogenous backbone (Punkt 2): yfinance gics_industry -> GICS Industry Group. The MARGIN-BLIND axis the dual-arm relative bucket rolls up to, replacing the multimodal GICS-sector catch-all. Only thin industries (those that do not clear n_min at their own industry level) need an entry; thick industries resolve at industry level before the group node is consulted, so their omission is harmless.
_meta.axis: GICS Industry Group (published taxonomy). NEVER hand-trim groups toward margin coherence — that turns the dispersion/unimodality acceptance gate into a rubber stamp (adaptive-stat-swallows-judgment). The gate, not this map, decides which group-buckets are valid; multimodal groups simply are not pinned in the median table -> fail-safe.
_meta.grounding: scripts/diagnose_industry_histogram.py over the 2026-06 cleaned universe (warm cache). Keys are the exact yfinance gics_industry strings observed thin (n<8).
_meta.review_flags: Yahoo's taxonomy is not GICS (spec section 4). The following assignments are genuine cross-taxonomy ambiguities resolved to the best published GICS group, NOT by margin — confirm or override: 'Education & Training Services' (Yahoo->Consumer Defensive; GICS-> Commercial & Professional Services vs Consumer Services), 'Pharmaceutical Retailers' (Health Care vs Consumer Staples Distribution & Retail), 'Pollution & Treatment Controls' (Commercial & Professional Services vs Capital Goods), 'Rental & Leasing Services' (Capital Goods/Trading Companies vs Commercial & Professional Services), 'Solar' (Yahoo->Technology; GICS often Industrials/Semis), 'Recreational Vehicles' (Automobiles & Components vs Consumer Durables & Apparel). The dispersion gate catches a mis-assignment that makes a bucket multimodal, but NOT one that lands a name in a spuriously-coherent wrong group — these six need a human taxonomy call.
